subject: Anthropology
title: Language Death
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Language Death.

hook.text: A language dies every two weeks. When the last speaker falls silent, an entire worldview vanishes forever.

buildup.text: Language death occurs when a community stops transmitting its language to the next generation.

discovery.text: Of roughly 7,000 languages today, nearly half are endangered. Most have fewer than 10,000 speakers.

twist.text: Languages don't die naturally — they're killed by colonialism, economic pressure, and policies that punish speakers.

climax.text: Each lost language erases unique knowledge about plants, weather, history, and ways of understanding the world.

punchline.text: A lost language is a lost way of seeing.

quiz.question: What primarily causes language death?
options: Colonialism, economic pressure, and suppressive policies
